url.description:
URL of a remote A2A server. When set, Card and Responses are ignored
and the agent is discovered from the remote /.well-known/agent.json.

auth.description: Auth configures authentication for remote A2A servers.

headers.description: Headers are static headers sent on every request to the remote server.

headers_from_env.description:
HeadersFromEnv injects headers from environment variables.
Format: "Header-Name=ENV_VAR_NAME".

timeout_ms.description: TimeoutMs sets the per-request timeout in milliseconds.

skill_filter.description: SkillFilter controls which skills from this agent are exposed.

A2AAgentConfig.description: A2AAgentConfig defines an A2A agent for Arena testing.

token.description: Token is the literal token value.

token_env.description: TokenEnv is the name of an environment variable containing the token.

A2AAuthConfig.description: A2AAuthConfig configures authentication for a remote A2A agent.

A2ACardConfig.description: A2ACardConfig defines the agent card for a mock A2A agent.

A2AMatchConfig.description: A2AMatchConfig defines how to match incoming messages for a mock A2A agent.

A2APartConfig.description: A2APartConfig defines a single response part for a mock A2A agent.

A2AResponseConfig.description: A2AResponseConfig defines the response parts for a mock A2A agent.

A2AResponseRule.description: A2AResponseRule defines a response rule for a mock A2A agent.

A2ASkillConfig.description: A2ASkillConfig defines a single skill in a mock A2A agent card.

A2ASkillFilter.description: A2ASkillFilter controls which skills from an A2A agent are exposed.

type.description: Handler type. PromptKit-known types are suggested, but values are not restricted to this list — a different runtime may define additional types.

params.description:
Params is optional: many assertion types take no parameters, so an
entry with only `type:` is valid and must not require an empty
`params: {}` stub. omitempty also drops it from the schema's required set.

pass_threshold.description:
PassThreshold is the minimum pass rate (0.0-1.0) required across trials.
Only meaningful when the parent scenario has Trials > 1.
If unset, defaults to 1.0 (must pass every trial).

AssertionConfig.description: AssertionConfig represents an assertion configuration used in arena scenarios.

tool_called.description: ToolCalled requires an exact tool name to have been called.

tool_called_pattern.description: ToolCalledPattern is a regex that must match at least one tool name.

any_tool_called.description: AnyToolCalled requires at least one tool to have been called.

min_tool_calls.description: MinToolCalls is the minimum number of tool calls required.

AssertionWhen.description: AssertionWhen specifies preconditions that must be met for an assertion to run.

enabled.description:
Enabled controls when monitoring is active. One of "auto", "on", "off".
Defaults to "auto" when unset.

rate.description:
Rate is the canonical sample rate inside the router. Must be one of
16000, 24000, or 48000. Defaults to 24000 when unset.

AudioMonitorSection.description: AudioMonitorSection configures real-time audio monitoring for duplex scenarios.

tool_failures.description: ToolFailures injects failures into specific tool calls.

ChaosConfig.description: ChaosConfig defines fault injection rules for a turn.

tool.description: Tool is the name of the tool to inject failures for.

mode.description: Mode is the type of failure: "error", "timeout", or "slow".

probability.description: Probability is the chance of failure (0.0 to 1.0, default 1.0).

message.description: Message is an optional custom error message.

ChaosToolFailure.description: ChaosToolFailure defines a failure injection rule for a specific tool.

globals.description:
Globals holds arena-level cross-cutting config that applies to
every scenario in addition to its own definitions. Distinct from
Defaults (which are "values when unspecified") — Globals is for
"always-additive" entries.

runtime.description:
Runtime carries a runtime configuration spec passed straight through to the
runtime layer (hooks, sandboxes, …). Arena wraps the runtime, so anything
the runtime config supports is available here under `runtime:` without
Arena needing a bespoke field for each.

provider_specs.description: Inline resource specs (alternative to file refs, merged into LoadedX during load)

tts_providers.description:
TTSProviders / STTProviders / EmbeddingProviders / ImageProviders are
the legacy role-specific slots. They still load correctly — every
entry's `role:` is validated against the slot — but the preferred
shape is a single unified `providers:` list where the loader routes
each provider into the right Loaded* map based on its `role:` value.
Mixing the legacy slots and the unified list is supported during
migration; both populate the same Loaded* maps.

voices.description:
Voices binds voice IDs to loaded TTS provider IDs. Personas reference
voice IDs (not provider IDs) so the same persona can run against a
real Cartesia voice in recording mode and a mock TTS provider in CI
just by editing this list.

Config.description: Config represents the main configuration structure

ContextMetadata.description: ContextMetadata provides structured context information for scenarios

token_budget.description: TokenBudget is the maximum tokens for context (0 = unlimited)

reserve_for_output.description: ReserveForOutput reserves tokens for the response (default 4000)

relevance.description: Relevance configures embedding-based truncation when Strategy is "relevance"

ContextPolicy.description: ContextPolicy defines context management for a scenario

run_timeout.description: RunTimeout is the per-run timeout duration (e.g. "5m", "30s"). Defaults to 5m.

config_dir.description:
ConfigDir is the base directory for all config files (prompts, providers, scenarios, tools).
If not set, defaults to the directory containing the main config file.
If the main config file path is not known, defaults to current working directory.

monitor.description: Monitor groups runtime-monitor configuration (audio, future surfaces).

inference.description:
Inference selects which configured inference client (under
spec.inference) serves each task when an assertion or handler doesn't
pin one explicitly. IDs reference entries in cfg.Inference.

html_report.description: Deprecated fields for backward compatibility (will be removed)

config.description: Config holds provider-specific configuration (opaque to core)

DeployConfig.description: DeployConfig represents deployment configuration for the arena

config.description: Config holds environment-specific provider configuration (merged with top-level config)

timeout.description: Timeout is the maximum session duration (e.g., "10m", "5m30s").

turn_detection.description: TurnDetection configures how turn boundaries are detected.

resilience.description: Resilience configures error handling and retry behavior.

DuplexConfig.description: DuplexConfig enables duplex (bidirectional) streaming mode for a scenario.

max_retries.description:
MaxRetries is the number of retry attempts for failed turns (default: 0).
Each retry creates a new session if the previous one ended.

retry_delay_ms.description: RetryDelayMs is the delay in milliseconds between retries (default: 1000).

partial_success_min_turns.description:
PartialSuccessMinTurns is the minimum completed turns to accept partial success (default: 1).
If the session ends unexpectedly but this many turns completed, treat as success.

ignore_last_turn_session_end.description:
IgnoreLastTurnSessionEnd treats session end on the final turn as success (default: true).
Useful when providers may close sessions after completing the expected conversation.

DuplexResilienceConfig.description: DuplexResilienceConfig configures error handling and retry behavior for duplex streaming.

platform.title: Platform
platform.description: Hyperscaler platform hosting for keyless auth (azure/bedrock/vertex)

Eval.description: Eval describes an evaluation configuration for replaying and validating saved conversations

assertions.description: Assertions to apply to each assistant message

EvalAllTurnsConfig.description: EvalAllTurnsConfig contains the assertions for all turns

type.description: Handler type. PromptKit-known types are suggested, but values are not restricted to this list — a different runtime may define additional types.

EvalRef.description: EvalRef references an eval configuration file

all_turns.description: Assertions to apply to all assistant messages

EvalTurnConfig.description: EvalTurnConfig configures turn-level assertions for evaluations Uses a structure parallel to Scenario turns but with all_turns instead of role

sandbox.title: Sandbox
sandbox.description: Name of a sandbox declared under spec.sandboxes

sandbox.title: Sandbox
sandbox.description: Name of a sandbox declared under spec.sandboxes

path.description: Optional: relative path to fragment file

FragmentRef.description: FragmentRef references a reusable prompt fragment Reuses the same structure as prompt.FragmentRef for consistency

conversation_assertions.description:
ConversationAssertions are appended to every scenario's own
conversation_assertions list at evaluation time. Useful for the
"DRY across scenarios" case (e.g. capture a metric on every run).
For pass/fail gates that must apply to all scenarios — though if
a gate truly belongs everywhere, an eval (in pack_evals) is the
more honest expression.

Globals.description: Globals holds arena-level cross-cutting config that applies to every scenario in addition to its own definitions.

HTMLOutputConfig.description: HTMLOutputConfig contains configuration options for HTML output

JSONOutputConfig.description: JSONOutputConfig contains configuration options for JSON output

JUnitOutputConfig.description: JUnitOutputConfig contains configuration options for JUnit XML output

JudgeDefaults.description: JudgeDefaults configures default judge prompt selection.

provider.description: Provider ID reference (must exist in spec.providers)

JudgeRef.description: JudgeRef references a judge configuration mapped to a provider.

JudgeSpec.description: JudgeSpec defines an inline judge configuration.

defaultLevel.title: Default Level
defaultLevel.description: Default log level for all modules

commonFields.title: Common Fields
commonFields.description: Key-value pairs added to every log entry

transport.title: MCP Transport
transport.description: Explicit transport adapter. Defaults: url→sse for back-compat; command→stdio. Set to 'streamable_http' to opt into the MCP 2025-03-26 Streamable HTTP transport.

source.title: MCPSource Name
source.description: Name of a host-registered MCPSource that provisions the endpoint per scope (e.g. 'docker'). Mutually exclusive with command and url.

scope.title: Source Scope
scope.description: Lifecycle boundary at which the source opens and closes. Required when source is set.

source_args.title: Source Args
source_args.description: Opaque arguments passed to the named MCPSource. Schema is source-specific. See the source's reference for accepted fields.

MarkdownOutputConfig.description: MarkdownOutputConfig contains configuration options for markdown output formatting

name.title: Name
name.description: Module name pattern using dot notation (e.g. runtime.pipeline)

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: Additional fields added to logs from this module

OutputConfig.description: OutputConfig contains configuration for all output formats

PersonaDefaults.description: PersonaDefaults contains default LLM parameters for the persona.

expressive.description:
Expressive opts the persona into TTS characterization markup. When
true, [UserPersonaPack.BuildSystemPrompt] prepends a bracket-tag
rubric (from the resolved TTS provider, or from
CharacterizationRubric below) so the LLM emits "[whispers]" /
"[excited]" / "[laughs]" etc. that downstream provider adapters
lower into their native dialects. Default false — existing personas
produce byte-identical prompts.

characterization_rubric.description:
CharacterizationRubric is an optional override that replaces the
provider-default rubric verbatim. Only consulted when Expressive is
true. Use this when a persona has unusual delivery requirements
(custom tag examples, narrower vocabulary, alternative phrasing).

PersonaStyle.description: PersonaStyle defines behavioral characteristics for the user persona

PromptConfigRef.description: PromptConfigRef references a prompt builder configuration

ProviderRef.description: ProviderRef references a provider configuration file

RecordingConfig.description: RecordingConfig contains configuration for session recording.

type.description: Recording format type hint (auto-detected if omitted)

RecordingSource.description: RecordingSource specifies where to load the saved conversation from

provider.description: Provider specifies the embedding provider: "openai" or "gemini"

model.description: Model optionally overrides the default embedding model for the provider

min_recent_messages.description:
MinRecentMessages always keeps the N most recent messages regardless of relevance.
Default: 3

always_keep_system_role.description:
AlwaysKeepSystemRole keeps all system role messages regardless of score.
Default: true

similarity_threshold.description:
SimilarityThreshold is the minimum score (0.0-1.0) to consider a message relevant.
Messages below this threshold are dropped first. Default: 0.0 (no threshold)

query_source.description:
QuerySource determines what text to compare messages against.
Values: "last_user" (default), "last_n", "custom"

last_n_count.description:
LastNCount is the number of messages to use when QuerySource is "last_n".
Default: 3

custom_query.description: CustomQuery is the query text when QuerySource is "custom".

cache_embeddings.description:
CacheEmbeddings enables caching of embeddings across truncation calls.
Default: false

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Tool implementation bindings keyed by tool name

evals.title: Evals
evals.description: External eval process bindings keyed by eval type name

sandboxes.title: Sandboxes
sandboxes.description: Named sandbox backends for exec-hook subprocess launch

selectors.title: Selectors
selectors.description: External selector processes narrowing skill and tool candidate sets

tool_selector.title: ToolSelector
tool_selector.description: Name of a selector declared under spec.selectors used to narrow the LLM-visible tool set per turn

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Registered sandbox factory name (direct ships in-tree; docker_run/docker_exec/kubectl_exec are examples)

providers.description: ProvidersOverride: If empty, uses all arena providers.

required_capabilities.description:
RequiredCapabilities filters providers to only those supporting all listed capabilities.
Valid values: text, streaming, vision, tools, json, audio, video, documents

streaming.description: Enable streaming for all turns by default.

context_policy.description: Context management policy for long conversations.

conversation_assertions.description: Assertions evaluated after the entire conversation completes.

duplex.description: Duplex enables bidirectional streaming mode for voice/audio scenarios.

voice.description:
Voice references an arena-level voice id (see Config.Voices) used to
synthesize this scenario's scripted-text user turns. The duplex executor
resolves the voice via Config.ResolveVoice.

For selfplay scenarios (turns with role: selfplay-user) the persona owns
voice choice; Scenario.Voice is ignored.

variables.description: Template variables to inject into the pack

trials.description: Number of times to run this scenario for statistical evaluation

seed_memories.description:
SeedMemories pre-populates the memory store before the first turn.
Uses the same fields as memory__remember: content (required), type, confidence, metadata.

Scenario.description: Scenario describes user turns, context, and validation constraints.

SeedMemoryEntry.description: SeedMemoryEntry defines a memory to pre-populate before scenario execution.

sandbox.title: Sandbox
sandbox.description: Name of a sandbox declared under spec.sandboxes

enabled.description:
Deprecated: Enabled is redundant — self-play is enabled whenever this section
is present. The field is retained for backward compatibility and will be removed
in a future release. To disable self-play, remove the self_play section entirely.

provider.description: Provider ID reference (must exist in spec.providers)

SelfPlayRoleGroup.description: SelfPlayRoleGroup defines user LLM configuration for self-play

selector.title: Selector
selector.description: Name of a selector declared under spec.selectors

max_rounds.description:
MaxRounds is the maximum number of tool-call rounds per turn.
0/unset means use the Arena default (50). Never unlimited.

max_cost_usd.description:
MaxCostUSD is the maximum cost in USD allowed per turn.
0/unset means use the Arena default ($2.00). Never unlimited.

max_identical_tool_calls.description:
MaxIdenticalToolCalls is the maximum number of times the same tool may be
called with identical arguments before the loop is aborted.
0/unset means use the Arena default (3). Never unlimited.

ToolPolicy.description: ToolPolicy defines constraints for tool usage in scenarios

ToolRef.description: ToolRef references a tool configuration file

mode.description: Execution mode. One of: 'mock' (use mock_result or mock_template) - 'live' (HTTP via 'http') - 'mcp' (MCP server) - 'exec' (subprocess) - 'client' (client-side handler).

mock_result.description: Static mock response returned regardless of tool-call args. Use when the response does not depend on inputs. Mutually exclusive with mock_template.

mock_template.description: Go text/template rendered against tool-call args (parsed as a JSON map). Rendered output is parsed back as JSON. Use this instead of mock_result when the response should depend on inputs (e.g. branching on order_id with {{ if eq .order_id "X" }}...{{ end }}). Mutually exclusive with mock_result.

TurnContentPart.description: TurnContentPart represents a content part in a scenario turn (simplified for YAML configuration)

role.description: "user", "assistant", or provider selector like "claude-user" (only for self-play turns)

parts.description:
Multimodal content parts (text, images, audio, video)
If Parts is non-empty, it takes precedence over Content.

persona.description: Self-play specific fields (when role is a provider selector like "claude-user")

turns.description: Turns is the number of self-play exchanges. Exact count if MaxTurns absent.

max_turns.description: MaxTurns is the upper bound; enables natural termination when > Turns.

streaming.description: Streaming control - if nil, uses scenario-level streaming setting

consent_overrides.description:
ConsentOverrides controls consent simulation for client-side tools.
Keys are tool names, values are "grant", "deny", or "timeout".

perturbations.description:
Perturbations define variable substitutions for behavioral testing.
Each key is a placeholder name (e.g., "city") and the value is a list of variants.
The engine expands each variant into a separate trial, substituting {key} in Content.

chaos.description: Chaos configures fault injection for resilience testing.

TurnDefinition.description: TurnDefinition represents a single conversation turn definition

mode.description: Mode specifies the turn detection method: "vad" (voice activity detection) or "asm" (provider-native).

vad.description: VAD contains voice activity detection settings (used when Mode is "vad").

TurnDetectionConfig.description: TurnDetectionConfig configures turn detection for duplex mode.

file_path.description: Relative path to media file (resolved at test time)

TurnMediaContent.description: TurnMediaContent represents media content in a turn definition

voice.description:
Voice references an arena-level voice id (see Config.Voices). When
set, selfplay synthesis routes this persona's text through the bound
TTS provider. Empty means the arena falls back to its default voice
or fails fast if no default is configured.

UserPersonaPack.description: UserPersonaPack defines how the User LLM behaves in self-play scenarios

silence_threshold_ms.description: SilenceThresholdMs is the silence duration in milliseconds to trigger turn end (default: 500).

min_speech_ms.description: MinSpeechMs is the minimum speech duration before silence counts (default: 1000).

max_turn_duration_s.description: MaxTurnDurationS forces turn end after this duration in seconds (default: 60).

VADConfig.description: VADConfig configures voice activity detection for turn detection.

title: PromptArena Configuration
description: Main configuration for PromptArena test suites
